ops.title: codex-ask developer-instructions die: root cause + fix (v1.3.5)
ops.body:
Lanes intermittently died with `codex-ask: cannot read developer instructions at /opt/homebrew/Caskroom/codex-ask/AGENTS.md` (reported 2026-07-22 as the "spec-fixes lane" failure).

**Root cause (structural, not transient):** `readAgentsMd()` (plugins/codex/dispatch.go:237#vrvk) resolved AGENTS.md only from the binary's own location — grandparent of the unresolved `os.Executable()`, then grandparent of the symlink-resolved `selfPath` — and died otherwise. The brew cask (`yasyf/tap/codex-ask`, a cask since v1.0.1) ships only the bare binary, so any invocation reaching the brew binary outside a plugin-root `bin/` symlink died. Lanes invoke bare `codex-ask`, so per-session PATH order decided survival (`/opt/homebrew/bin` can shadow the plugin `bin/`).

**Trigger:** the plugin's own provisioning (install-binary.sh arm 3) ran `brew upgrade` at 2026-07-22 19:01:49 when the marketplace pin moved to 1.3.4, flipping what bare `codex-ask` resolves to mid-session.

**Fix (v1.3.5):** (1) `//go:embed AGENTS.md` fallback in `readAgentsMd` — disk lookups stay first as dev/plugin-root overrides, the embedded copy makes the dev feed invocation-path-independent; (2) printed `AWAIT:`/`--collect` recovery recipes use the unresolved invocation path instead of the resolved Caskroom-versioned path (deleted by every brew upgrade); (3) SKILL.md + wrapper agents invoke `"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bin/codex-ask"` explicitly.

**Sibling hazard to remember:** any durable artifact that captures a symlink-*resolved* Homebrew Caskroom/Cellar path dies on the next upgrade; capture the stable symlink path instead.
